title: Focaccia in Three Parts
excerpt: Ingredients Focaccia 1 kg store-bought dough olive oil white wine vinegar coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper Baby Tomato & Basil Part 250 ml baby tomatoes, halved 125 ml basil leaves, roughly torn 2 large, thinly sliced garlic cloves Three Cheeses & Rosemary Part 125 ml thin slices …

seo.title: Focaccia in Three Parts - Marinda Kook

html:
Ingredients

Focaccia

  • 1 kg store-bought dough
  • olive oil
  • white wine vinegar
  • coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper

Baby Tomato & Basil Part

  • 250 ml baby tomatoes, halved
  • 125 ml basil leaves, roughly torn
  • 2 large, thinly sliced garlic cloves

Three Cheeses & Rosemary Part

  • 125 ml thin slices Taleggio cheese (mozzarella will also do, if it must)
  • 100 g log of goat's milk cheese, broken into small pieces
  • 125 ml coarsely grated Parmesan cheese
  • 15 ml coarsely chopped rosemary

Classic Part (quantities to taste)

  • smoked mozzarella slices
  • thin garlic slices
  • anchovy fillets
  • pitted black olives
  • dried oregano

Method

Grease a baking tray with butter or spray with non-stick spray. Press the dough into the pan so that it covers the whole surface. Prick the dough with a fork and bake for 8 minutes at 180°C.

Arrange the ingredients above, to taste, in three separate parts over the dough. Drizzle with olive oil and vinegar to taste and season with coarse salt and freshly ground black pepper.

Bake for a further 15 minutes at 180°C or until cooked through.

Serves 6 – 8 with a salad, soup or as a nibble with drinks

Tip: Sprinkle any of the parts (or all three) with dried red chilli flakes. And if you feel like it, chop generous amounts of fresh herbs over it.

Easy!
